page.content: In the fiscal year 2024, Stripe Inc. reported significant metrics across both merchant fees and lending platform origination, encapsulated in two comprehensive tables. For the merchant fees, the total transaction volume was 500,000, generating total revenue of $120M with 15,000 active merchants and 2,500 new merchants, while exhibiting a refund rate of 0.5% and a chargeback rate of 1.2%. In another segment, transaction volume reached 350,000, yielding total revenue of $90M with 25,000 active merchants and 3,000 new merchants, alongside a refund rate of 0.8% and a chargeback rate of 1.5%. The third entry recorded a transaction volume of 650,000, resulting in total revenue of $150M, comprising 20,000 active merchants and 4,000 new merchants, with a refund rate of 0.4% and a chargeback rate of 1.0%. Moving to lending platform metrics, the P/E ratio for Q1 was 15.2, with a current ratio of 1.8, a debt/equity ratio of 0.5, a return on equity of 12.5%, a net profit margin of 20.3%, a loan default rate of 2.1%, a customer acquisition cost of $5,000, and an average loan size of $5,000. For Q2, the P/E ratio was 14.8, current ratio 2.0, debt/equity 0.6, return on equity 11.8%, net profit margin 21.0%, loan default rate 2.3%, customer acquisition cost $5,250, and average loan size $5,250.

lines.text: In the fiscal year 2024, Stripe Inc. reported significant metrics across both merchant fees and lending platform origination,
encapsulated in two comprehensive tables. For the merchant fees, the total transaction volume was 500,000, generating total
revenue of $120M with 15,000 active merchants and 2,500 new merchants, while exhibiting a refund rate of 0.5% and a
chargeback rate of 1.2%. In another segment, transaction volume reached 350,000, yielding total revenue of $90M with 25,000
active merchants and 3,000 new merchants, alongside a refund rate of 0.8% and a chargeback rate of 1.5%. The third entry
recorded a transaction volume of 650,000, resulting in total revenue of $150M, comprising 20,000 active merchants and 4,000
new merchants, with a refund rate of 0.4% and a chargeback rate of 1.0%. Moving to lending platform metrics, the P/E ratio for
Q1 was 15.2, with a current ratio of 1.8, a debt/equity ratio of 0.5, a return on equity of 12.5%, a net profit margin of 20.3%, a
loan default rate of 2.1%, a customer acquisition cost of $5,000, and an average loan size of $5,000. For Q2, the P/E ratio was
14.8, current ratio 2.0, debt/equity 0.6, return on equity 11.8%, net profit margin 21.0%, loan default rate 2.3%, customer
acquisition cost $5,250, and average loan size $5,250.

page.content: In Q3, these figures shifted to a P/E ratio of 16.0, current ratio 1.9, debt/equity 0.4, return on equity 13.1%, net profit margin 19.5%, loan default rate 1.9%, customer acquisition cost $5,500, and average loan size $5,500. Finally, Q4 recorded a P/E ratio of 15.5, current ratio 1.7, debt/equity 0.7, return on equity 12.0%, net profit margin 20.0%, loan default rate 2.0%, customer acquisition cost $5,750, and average loan size $5,750.

lines.text: In Q3, these figures shifted to a P/E ratio of 16.0, current ratio 1.9, debt/equity 0.4, return on equity 13.1%, net profit margin
19.5%, loan default rate 1.9%, customer acquisition cost $5,500, and average loan size $5,500. Finally, Q4 recorded a P/E ratio
of 15.5, current ratio 1.7, debt/equity 0.7, return on equity 12.0%, net profit margin 20.0%, loan default rate 2.0%, customer
acquisition cost $5,750, and average loan size $5,750.

page.content: Overall, the total metrics for the lending platform indicated a P/E ratio of 15.3, a current ratio of 1.85, a debt/equity ratio of 0.5, a return on equity of 12.4%, a net profit margin of 20.2%, a loan default rate of 2.08%, and an average loan size of $5,437. The quarterly growth metrics showed a growth of 10% in Q1, 15% in Q2, with yearly growth at 8%, loan volume at $50M, and net revenue of $100M, alongside a customer growth of 5,000.
